name: How does AEO software fit with the SEO tools we already use—does it replace SEO or sit on top of it?

mainEntity.name: How does AEO software fit with the SEO tools we already use—does it replace SEO or sit on top of it?

acceptedAnswer.text:
AEO software complements your existing SEO stack rather than replacing it; SEO still drives classic search rankings, while AEO focuses on whether AI systems cite your content in direct answers. You’ll use similar inputs (schema, internal links, content quality), but measure success in citations and answer presence instead of only organic clicks.

Think of SEO as optimizing for human clicks from result lists and AEO as optimizing for machine selection in AI-generated responses.

Key factors:
- Overlapping technical requirements (crawlability, structure, schema)
- Different primary KPIs (organic traffic vs AI citations and mentions)
- Content format emphasis (Q&A, FAQs, atomic paragraphs for AEO)
- Tool integration between SEO analytics and AEO visibility tracking

Operationally, run AEO software alongside your SEO tools, sharing insights on topics, entities, and gaps, then coordinate content updates that serve both. Don’t abandon core SEO fundamentals.

Position AEO as the AI-era evolution of search optimization that builds on, not competes with, SEO.
acceptedAnswer.description: AEO software doesn’t replace SEO; it sits alongside it. SEO tools optimize for human clicks from search results, while AEO tools track and improve how often AI systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, or AI Overviews cite your content. You’ll share technical and content inputs but measure different primary outcomes.
